displayTitle: The Dose Record That Outlives Every Job

beats.narration: Your dose record is the one document that follows you for an entire career. It never resets — and biology is the reason it matters.

payload.title: THE RECORD FOLLOWS YOU
payload.sub: Dose accumulates for life. Tissue decides why it counts.

beats.narration: Each period your badge is processed into your dose of record. Those numbers never reset at year end — they add into a lifetime cumulative total.

tiles.s: Year end does not erase it

beats.narration: The limit is yours, not the job's. Change employers and the new one requests your prior dose, so the count carries forward unbroken.

payload.heading: The limit attaches to the person

beats.narration: Why it matters: radiation does not treat tissue alike. By Bergonie and Tribondeau, fast-dividing, immature cells are most sensitive — marrow, gonads, gut lining.
Mature muscle, bone, and nerve resist it. That ranking is exactly why the whole-body limit runs ten times stricter than the skin limit.

payload.sub: Whole-body 5 rem, skin 50 — biology wrote the gap.
